title: One-Board Wooden Centerpiece Tray
summary: A long, narrow centerpiece tray for a rectangular or oval dining table, built entirely from one board.
description:
A round bowl centerpiece doesn't suit a long rectangular or oval table — it either looks lost in the middle or blocks sightlines across a narrow width. This tray solves that by being long and narrow on purpose, built entirely from a single board so its proportions naturally match a runner-style table rather than a round one.

Every piece — sides and bottom — comes from **one board**, which keeps the grain and color consistent across the whole tray instead of showing a seam between mismatched stock.

Cabinet pulls on each end turn it into something liftable and movable, rather than a fixed centerpiece you have to nudge around by its edges.

steps.title: Cut the tray pieces
steps.body: Cut the single 1x12 board down into the tray's side and bottom pieces, sized to the length of your table.

steps.title: Chamfer the edges (optional)
steps.body: Optionally rout a chamfered edge along the top edges of the side pieces for a more finished look.

steps.title: Glue up the tray
steps.body: Glue and clamp the pieces together and let them cure for about an hour.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand the entire assembly smooth, including the bottom, then stain or paint as desired.

steps.title: Add the handles
steps.body: Attach two cabinet pulls to the ends for handles.
